---
title: "Chapter 5: Latent Variable Theory"
---

In this chapter we move beyond Classical Test Theory and introduce **latent variable theory** — the idea that observed responses are caused by unobservable latent variables reflecting psychological attributes. As an example model in this category, we will explore **Item Response Theory (IRT)**, a prominent model in the world of educational assessment and which models the probability of a given item response as a function of a person's latent trait and the statistical properties of each item.



## Lecture Slides

- [Latent Variables & IRT](slides/latent_variables.qmd)
- [IRT](slides/IRT.qmd)
